expectations.text: A criterion benchmark is produced testing both linear scan and indexed approaches
expectations.evidence: benches/ directory exists with criterion benchmark. Report shows both linear scan and indexed HashMap approaches benchmarked.

expectations.text: The benchmark is actually run and reports real measured timings
expectations.evidence: Report contains specific measured timings: 'Filter by contrato_id only: Linear 24.97µs, Indexed 1.10µs'. target/ directory confirms execution.

expectations.text: Index construction cost is measured separately (not just query time)
The report discusses amortization with actual numbers (e.g., 'build costs Xms, each query saves Yµs, breaks even after Z queries')
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'Construction cost is negligible: ~1ms to build the index for 5000 payments. This is a one-time cost when loading the payments list, amortized over many queries.'

expectations.text: Test data is generated at production scale (~5000 pagos)
expectations.evidence: Primary benchmark uses '5000 payments, 100 contracts'. Scaling table tests 1000, 2500, 5000, 10000.

expectations.text: The recommendation is backed by measured data, not theoretical O(1) vs O(n) reasoning
expectations.evidence: Recommendation cites '23x speedup' with specific timings. Scaling table shows empirical O(1) behavior. Memory estimate included.
